description: Visually verifiable camera-angle and camera-stream commands over DataChannel.
notes: Use screenshots plus proof.webm as primary evidence.
Run first; this is the fastest confidence check for session attach plus command transport.

description: Emotion blend/override commands with visible face-state changes.
notes: Requires human or frame-level semantic review because the change may be subtle.
Use a stable medium or close camera before batch start.

description: Preset-level customization commands expected to cause visible avatar changes.
notes: Prefer low-count subsets first because some commands may stack or persist between runs.
Reset or re-open a fresh session between sub-batches if visual state drift becomes confusing.

description: Post-processing commands expected to change exposure, grain, vignette, and lens characteristics.
notes: Frame diffs matter more than obvious gross motion here.
Batch should be split further if changes become too hard to attribute command-by-command.

description: Lighting and build-your-own scene commands with visible world-state changes.
notes: Some commands are templates and require parameterized expansion before execution.
Start with non-destructive or reset-friendly scene commands.

description: BYOB and TTS timing contract commands.
notes: Requires game diagnostics and/or audible proof, not just screenshots.
Timing commands should be validated as control-state expectations, not avatar-content changes.

description: ElevenLabs public command contract, likely requiring credentials or mock/no-op handling.
notes: Do not assume credentials are available.
Treat this as contract validation first, not guaranteed successful synthesis.
